title: Consolidate env handling into pkg/env
body:
Taking this one over from @vrothberg so he can enjoy his vacation

Original description from #5264 -
Env-variable related code is scattered across several packages making it hard to maintain and extend.  Consolidate the code into a new pkg/env package.

I modified this a bit to remove the changes to oci_conmon_linux.go as the that code tried to fix was actually a crun issue.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Weird. One of the flakes is in `Cirrus CI / special_testing_rootless TEST_REMOTE_CLIENT:false`, in `podman run --host-env environment test`:
```
test/e2e/run_test.go:253

Running: podman [options] --events-backend file --storage-driver vfs run --rm --env-host docker.io/library/alpine:latest printenv FOO
Error: executable file not found in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
```
It seems to fail three times in a row. I can't reproduce it, not on f30 or f30, not root or rootless. not with multiple settings of my `$PATH`. I can't tell from the CI name if this is f30 or f31.

@mheon @baude here's a smoking-gun [failure of the duplicate-ip test](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/6347871272304640/logs/integration_test.log#t--Podman-run-two-containers-with-the-same-IP) showing that container 2 really is running and has the expected IP as container 1.

Other failures in that log are the same printenv PATH flate.

Whoa! I'm seeing two different failure messages here, on two different runs!

[First one](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/6629346249015296/logs/integration_test.log#t--podman-run---host-env-environment-test)
```
Error: executable file not found in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
```
[Second](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/6347871272304640/logs/integration_test.log#t--podman-run---host-env-environment-test)
```
Error: container_linux.go:349: starting container process caused "exec: \"printenv\": executable file not found in $PATH": OCI runtime command not found error
```
The second one (properly and helpfully) includes `printenv` as the not-found command. Could this be a crun vs runc difference? Can we make them *both* emit the proper form? And, even better, can we include the expansion of `$PATH` in all such messages?
@edsantiago I don't know if the env-host stuff is a flake - this PR does refactor that code. I can't replicate locally, so I've added a commit to see if something wierd is happening with $PATH.
As for the two errors... I bet that's crun vs runc (F30 vs F31)
Now, the real interesting part - it succeeds on Ubuntu.

`[+0494s] /root/bin:/var/tmp/go/bin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/var/tmp/go/bin`

That doesn't look to include `/bin` which is the cause of the issue. It looks like we're somehow copying in $PATH from the host - which `--env-host` should not do (and does not do on my system).
Alright, I'm giving up for now... this one makes no sense. I'll tag @vrothberg to take a look once he's back from vacation, see what he makes of this.
Since Matt is on PTO I opened https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/5367.
